name: Hero's Journey
description: The classic monomyth structure: call to adventure, trials, transformation, and return.

sections.title: Part I: Departure
sections.summary: The hero is introduced in their ordinary world and receives a call to adventure.

subsections.title: Chapter 1: The Ordinary World
subsections.summary: Establish the hero's everyday life, desires, and flaws.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 1: The Ordinary World

Describe the protagonist in their natural habitat ΓÇö their routines, relationships, and the subtle discontent that simmers beneath the surface...

subsections.title: Chapter 2: The Call to Adventure
subsections.summary: An event or message disrupts the status quo.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 2: The Call

Something changes. A letter arrives, a stranger appears, or an accident forces the first crack in their ordinary world...

subsections.title: Chapter 3: Crossing the Threshold
subsections.summary: The hero commits to leaving the familiar behind.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 3: Crossing the Threshold

The point of no return. The hero steps into the unknown, leaving safety behind forever...

sections.title: Part II: Initiation
sections.summary: Trials, allies, enemies, and the central ordeal.

subsections.title: Chapter 4: Tests and Allies
subsections.summary: The hero faces challenges and meets friends and foes.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 4: The Road of Trials

Each obstacle teaches the hero something. New allies are forged and enemies revealed...

subsections.title: Chapter 5: The Ordeal
subsections.summary: The central crisis ΓÇö a death and rebirth moment.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 5: The Central Ordeal

The hero faces their greatest fear. Something must die ΓÇö a belief, a relationship, or the hero themselves ΓÇö for transformation to occur...

sections.title: Part III: Return
sections.summary: The hero returns transformed, bringing a boon to their world.

subsections.title: Chapter 6: The Resurrection
subsections.summary: A final test that proves the hero's transformation.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 6: Resurrection

One last cleansing trial. The hero must apply everything they have learned in a definitive climactic confrontation...

subsections.title: Chapter 7: Return with the Elixir
subsections.summary: The hero comes home, changed, with something to share.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 7: The Return

Home is familiar yet different. The hero has grown and brings back wisdom, freedom, or peace to share with their community...

name: Three-Act Structure
description: Setup, Confrontation, Resolution ΓÇö a reliable dramatic arc for commercial fiction.

sections.title: Act I: Setup
sections.summary: Introduce characters, setting, and the inciting incident.

subsections.title: Chapter 1
subsections.summary: Hook the reader with character and conflict.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 1

Open with a moment that reveals character and hints at the central conflict. Show what's at stake...

subsections.title: Chapter 2
subsections.summary: The inciting incident disrupts everything.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 2

An event beyond the protagonist's control forces them to make a choice they can't take back...

sections.title: Act II: Confrontation
sections.summary: Rising stakes, obstacles, and the midpoint reversal.

subsections.title: Chapter 3
subsections.summary: Rising action and new challenges.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 3

The protagonist pursues their goal but faces escalating opposition. Alliances shift...

subsections.title: Chapter 4
subsections.summary: The midpoint crisis changes everything.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 4

A major revelation or reversal raises the stakes to a new level. The goal is redefined...

sections.title: Act III: Resolution
sections.summary: The climax and aftermath.

subsections.title: Chapter 5
subsections.summary: The final confrontation.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 5

Everything comes to a head. The protagonist must use every lesson learned to prevail...

subsections.title: Chapter 6
subsections.summary: The denouement ΓÇö tying loose ends.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 6

The dust settles. Show how the world and characters have changed, and hint at what comes next...

name: Feature Film Structure
description: A three-act screenplay outline with standard beat sheet.

sections.title: Act I ΓÇö The Setup
sections.summary: Establish the world, protagonist, and inciting incident within the first ~25 pages.

subsections.title: Opening Image
subsections.summary: A visual snapshot of the protagonist before the journey.
subsections.content:
**OPENING IMAGE**

A single image that captures who the protagonist is right now ΓÇö before everything changes.

[Scene description, character introduction, tone-setting moment]

subsections.title: Inciting Incident
subsections.summary: Around page 10-12, something happens that sets the story in motion.
subsections.content:
**INCITING INCIDENT**

An event outside the protagonist's control that creates a compelling question.

[Scene description, disruption of status quo]

sections.title: Act II ΓÇö The Confrontation
sections.summary: Rising action from page 25 to 85. The protagonist pursues their goal through escalating obstacles.

subsections.title: Midpoint
subsections.summary: Around page 55, a major twist raises the stakes.
subsections.content:
**MIDPOINT**

A revelation or reversal that changes the nature of the conflict. The stakes get personal.

[Scene description, major twist]

subsections.title: All Is Lost
subsections.summary: The lowest point around page 75.
subsections.content:
**ALL IS LOST**

The protagonist hits rock bottom. The goal seems impossible. A mentor figure may die or be lost.

[Scene description, dark night of the soul]

sections.title: Act III ΓÇö The Resolution
sections.summary: From page 85 to 110. The climax and aftermath.

subsections.title: Climax
subsections.summary: The final confrontation.
subsections.content:
**CLIMAX**

The protagonist faces the central conflict one last time. Everything they have learned is put to the test.

[Scene description, final confrontation]

subsections.title: Final Image
subsections.summary: A visual bookend showing how the protagonist has changed.
subsections.content:
**FINAL IMAGE**

The closing image of the film ΓÇö a mirror of the opening image that shows the transformation.

[Scene description, resolution, thematic closure]

name: Standard Invoice
description: A professional invoice template with itemized line items, tax, and payment terms.

sections.title: Header & Sender Info
sections.summary: Company details, invoice number, and dates.

subsections.title: Invoice Header
subsections.summary: Sender logo, business name, and contact details.
subsections.content:
# INVOICE

**[Your Company Name]**
[Street Address]
[City, State ZIP]
[Email] | [Phone]

---

subsections.title: Invoice Meta
subsections.summary: Invoice number, date, due date, and customer reference.
subsections.content:
**Invoice #**: [INV-001]
**Date**: [Date]
**Due Date**: [Date + 30 days]
**Customer ID**: [CUST-001]

sections.title: Bill To & Line Items
sections.summary: Customer details and itemized charges.

subsections.title: Bill To
subsections.summary: Customer name, company, and billing address.
subsections.content:
## Bill To

**[Customer Name]**
[Company Name]
[Billing Address]
[City, State ZIP]

subsections.title: Itemized Services
subsections.summary: List of products or services with quantities and rates.
subsections.content:
## Description of Services

| # | Description | Qty | Unit Price | Amount |
|---|-------------|-----|------------|--------|
| 1 | [Service description] | 1 | $0.00 | $0.00 |
| 2 | [Service description] | 1 | $0.00 | $0.00 |

sections.title: Totals & Payment Terms
sections.summary: Subtotal, tax, total due, and payment instructions.

subsections.title: Summary
subsections.summary: Financial totals.

subsections.title: Payment Terms
subsections.summary: Payment method, terms, and late fee policy.
subsections.content:
## Payment Terms

- **Payment Method**: Bank Transfer / Check / Credit Card
- **Payment Terms**: Net 30
- **Late Fee**: 1.5% per month on overdue balances

Thank you for your business!

name: Standard Pitch Deck
description: A persuasive presentation structure with problem, solution, market, and ask.

sections.title: Opening & Problem
sections.summary: Hook the audience and define the problem you solve.

subsections.title: Title Slide
subsections.summary: Project name, tagline, and presenter name.
subsections.content:
# [Presentation Title]

*[Tagline / One-liner]*

[Presenter Name] ΓÇö [Date]

subsections.title: The Problem
subsections.summary: What pain point or gap exists in the market?
subsections.content:
## The Problem

Describe the specific problem your audience faces. Use data, anecdotes, or visuals to make it tangible and urgent.

> "[Quote from a real customer or stakeholder]"

sections.title: Solution & Market
sections.summary: Present your solution, market size, and business model.

subsections.title: The Solution
subsections.summary: How your product or idea solves the problem.
subsections.content:
## Our Solution

Explain what you offer and why it's uniquely positioned to solve the problem. Highlight the key differentiator.

- **Feature A**: Benefit description
- **Feature B**: Benefit description
- **Feature C**: Benefit description

subsections.title: Market Opportunity
subsections.summary: TAM, SAM, SOM and competitive landscape.
subsections.content:
## Market Opportunity

- **TAM (Total Addressable Market)**: $X B
- **SAM (Serviceable Available Market)**: $X M
- **SOM (Serviceable Obtainable Market)**: $X M

**Competitive Advantage**: [Your unique edge over alternatives]

sections.title: Execution & Ask
sections.summary: Team, roadmap, and the specific ask.

subsections.title: Roadmap
subsections.summary: Timeline of milestones.
subsections.content:
## Roadmap

| Phase | Timeline | Milestone |
|-------|----------|-----------|
| Phase 1 | Q1 2026 | MVP Launch |
| Phase 2 | Q2 2026 | Scale & Optimize |
| Phase 3 | Q3 2026 | Market Expansion |

subsections.title: The Ask
subsections.summary: What you need from the audience.
subsections.content:
## The Ask

We are seeking **[amount]** to **[purpose]**.

- **Use of Funds**: [Breakdown]
- **Current Stage**: [Stage]
- **Previous Funding**: [Amount]

name: Welcome Email Sequence
description: A 3-email nurture sequence for onboarding new subscribers or customers.

sections.title: Email 1 ΓÇö Welcome & Value
sections.summary: First impression and immediate value delivery.

subsections.title: Subject Line Options
subsections.summary: Catchy subject lines for the welcome email.
subsections.content:
**Subject Line Options:**
- Welcome to [Brand]! Here's your first treat 
- You're in! Let's get started
- [Name], welcome to the community

---

subsections.title: Email Body
subsections.summary: Warm welcome, set expectations, deliver initial value.
subsections.content:
## Hi [First Name],

Welcome to **[Brand]**! We're thrilled to have you.

Here's what you can expect from us:
- **[Benefit 1]**: Brief explanation
- **[Benefit 2]**: Brief explanation
- **[Benefit 3]**: Brief explanation

**Your first resource**: [Link to valuable content]

Talk soon,
[Your Name]

sections.title: Email 2 ΓÇö Story & Connection
sections.summary: Share the brand story and build a deeper connection.

subsections.title: Subject Line Options
subsections.summary: Story-driven subject lines.
subsections.content:
**Subject Line Options:**
- The story behind [Brand]
- Why we started [Brand]
- [Name], a quick story for you

---

subsections.title: Email Body
subsections.summary: Founder story, mission, and values.
subsections.content:
## Hi [First Name],

We believe **[core belief]**.

That's why we started **[Brand]**. [2-3 sentences about the founder story / mission].

We're here to **[big picture goal]**.

Reply to this email ΓÇö I'd love to hear your story too.

[Your Name]

sections.title: Email 3 ΓÇö Offer & CTA
sections.summary: Present the primary offer with a clear call to action.

subsections.title: Subject Line Options
subsections.summary: Offer-focused subject lines.
subsections.content:
**Subject Line Options:**
- A special offer just for you, [Name]
- Your exclusive [Brand] discount inside
- [Name], ready to take the next step?

---

subsections.title: Email Body
subsections.summary: Offer presentation with social proof and clear CTA.
subsections.content:
## Hi [First Name],

By now you've seen what **[Brand]** is all about. Ready to take the next step?

**[Offer Name]** ΓÇö **[Brief description of what they get]**

> "[Testimonial from a happy customer]"

**Claim your offer now**: [CTA Button Link]

This exclusive offer expires **[date]**.

[Your Name]

name: Product User Guide
description: A comprehensive user guide structure with getting started, features, and troubleshooting.

sections.summary: Installation, setup, and first steps.

## Introduction

Welcome to **[Product Name]**! This guide will help you get the most out of [brief product description].

**Who this guide is for**: [Target audience description]

- **RAM**: [Minimum RAM]

### Overview
[What this feature does and when to use it]

2. **[Step 2]**: [Description]

sections.summary: Common issues and frequently asked questions.

subsections.summary: Solutions to frequently encountered problems.
subsections.content:

**Solution**: [Step-by-step fix]

### Issue: [Problem description]

subsections.summary: Frequently asked questions.
subsections.content:

A: [Answer]

**Q: [Question]?**
A: [Answer]

**Q: [Question]?**
A: [Answer]

---

*Need more help? Contact support at [email] or visit [help center URL]*

name: Product Launch Announcement
description: A journalist-friendly press release format for announcing a new product or milestone.

sections.title: Headline & Boilerplate
sections.summary: The hook, dateline, and company boilerplate.

subsections.title: Headline & Subhead
subsections.summary: Catchy headline with a supporting subhead.
subsections.content:
# [Catchy Headline: Company Name Announces Product Name]

*[Subhead: A one-sentence value proposition that expands on the headline]*

---

[CITY, State] ΓÇö [Date] ΓÇö [Company Name], a leader in [industry], today announced [announcement summary in 1-2 sentences].

subsections.title: Boilerplate
subsections.summary: Standard company description for the press release footer.
subsections.content:
### About [Company Name]

[Company Name] ([website URL]) is the leading provider of [brief company description]. Founded in [year], the company serves [customer base] across [locations/markets]. For more information, visit [website URL].

### Media Contact

[Name]
[Title]
[Email]
[Phone]

sections.title: Body & Quotes
sections.summary: The announcement details and executive quotes.

subsections.title: Announcement Body
subsections.summary: Details of what is being announced and why it matters.
subsections.content:
## [Detail Heading]

[2-3 paragraphs describing the announcement in detail. Cover the problem being solved, key features, and the impact on customers or the industry.]

**[Feature/benefit 1]**: [Description]
**[Feature/benefit 2]**: [Description]
**[Feature/benefit 3]**: [Description]

subsections.title: Executive Quote
subsections.summary: Quote from company leadership.
subsections.content:
> "[Compelling quote from CEO or executive about the announcement and its significance.]"
> ΓÇö **[Name]**, [Title], [Company Name]

[Optional: Additional quote from a customer, partner, or industry analyst]

sections.title: Availability & CTA
sections.summary: Pricing, availability, and the call to action.

subsections.title: Availability
subsections.summary: When and how to access the announced product.
subsections.content:
## Availability

**[Product Name]** is available [starting date / immediately] at [pricing details].

- **[Plan/Tier 1]**: $X ΓÇö [Description]
- **[Plan/Tier 2]**: $X ΓÇö [Description]

[Link to product page or press kit]

name: Research Grant Proposal
description: Standard NIH/NSF-style grant proposal structure with abstract, methodology, and budget.

sections.title: Abstract & Introduction
sections.summary: Project summary, specific aims, and background.

subsections.title: Project Abstract
subsections.summary: A concise summary of the proposed research (300 words).
subsections.content:
# [Project Title]

## Abstract

**Background**: [1-2 sentences on the research context]
**Objective**: [Specific aim or hypothesis]
**Methods**: [Brief description of approach]
**Expected Outcomes**: [Anticipated results and impact]
**Budget Request**: [$Amount over X years]

subsections.title: Specific Aims
subsections.summary: The core objectives of the proposed research.
subsections.content:
## Specific Aims

**Aim 1**: [Primary objective with measurable outcome]
**Aim 2**: [Secondary objective]
**Aim 3**: [Tertiary objective]

*Collectively, these aims will [broader impact statement].*

sections.title: Research Strategy
sections.summary: Methodology, timeline, and expected outcomes.

subsections.title: Methodology
subsections.summary: Detailed approach for each specific aim.
subsections.content:
## Research Strategy

### Aim 1: [Restate Aim 1]
**Approach**: [Detailed methodology]
**Data Analysis**: [Statistical methods]
**Expected Outcomes**: [What success looks like]
**Potential Pitfalls**: [Contingency plans]

### Aim 2: [Restate Aim 2]
**Approach**: [Detailed methodology]
**Data Analysis**: [Statistical methods]

subsections.title: Timeline
subsections.summary: Project timeline and milestones.
subsections.content:
## Timeline

| Year | Milestones |
|------|------------|
| Year 1 | [Milestone 1], [Milestone 2] |
| Year 2 | [Milestone 3], [Milestone 4] |
| Year 3 | [Milestone 5], [Final outcomes] |

sections.title: Budget & Biosketch
sections.summary: Budget justification and investigator qualifications.

subsections.title: Budget Justification
subsections.summary: Detailed breakdown of requested funds.
subsections.content:
## Budget

| Category | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 | Total |
|----------|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| Personnel | $X | $X | $X | $X |
| Equipment | $X | $X | $X | $X |
| Supplies | $X | $X | $X | $X |
| **Total** | **$X** | **$X** | **$X** | **$X** |

### Justification
[Explanation of major budget items]

subsections.title: Investigator Biosketch
subsections.summary: PI and co-investigator qualifications.
subsections.content:
## Principal Investigator: [Name]

**Title**: [Current position]
**Education**: [Degrees and institutions]
**Relevant Publications**: [3-5 key publications]
**Grant History**: [Previous funding experience]

name: Customer Case Study
description: A story-driven case study template with challenge, solution, and measurable results.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.summary: The customer, the challenge, and the outcome at a glance.

subsections.summary: Quick reference table.

|---|----|

[Customer Name], a leader in [industry], faced [specific challenge]. By partnering with [Our Company], they implemented [solution], achieving [key result] within [timeframe].

This case study explores the challenge, the approach taken, and the measurable outcomes delivered.

- [Pain point 1]
- [Pain point 2]
- [Pain point 3]

subsections.summary: The approach and implementation.

**Implementation**:
1. **[Phase 1]**: [Description]
2. **[Phase 2]**: [Description]
3. **[Phase 3]**: [Description]

subsections.content:
## What the Customer Says

> "[Powerful quote from the customer about their experience and the value delivered.]"
> ΓÇö **[Name]**, [Title], [Customer Company]

name: Standard Three-Act Story Structure
description: A classic story model divided into Setup, Confrontation, and Resolution.

sections.title: Act I: Setup
sections.summary: Introduce the protagonist, their ordinary world, and the inciting incident that calls them to action.

subsections.title: Chapter 1: The Ordinary World
subsections.summary: Establish the status quo and character motivations.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 1: The Ordinary World

Introduce the protagonist in their daily life. Show their personality, their flaws, and what they secretly long for...

subsections.title: Chapter 2: The Inciting Incident
subsections.summary: An event disrupts the ordinary world and forces the protagonist out of comfort.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 2: The Call to Adventure

A sudden shift occurs. The protagonist faces a choice, a threat, or a mystery that they cannot ignore...

sections.title: Act II: Confrontation
sections.summary: The protagonist enters the new world, faces rising stakes, obstacles, and the midpoint climax.

subsections.title: Chapter 3: Crossing the Threshold
subsections.summary: Protagonist commits to the journey and enters the unknown territory.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 3: Entering the Storm

There is no turning back. The boundaries of the old world fade behind them as they face their first trial...

subsections.title: Chapter 4: The Midpoint Climax
subsections.summary: A major revelation or shift in stakes changes the trajectory of the plot.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 4: The Pivot Point

An unexpected truth comes to light. The stakes are raised, and the protagonist must double their resolve...

sections.title: Act III: Resolution
sections.summary: The climax, the dark night of the soul, and the ultimate resolution of the story arc.

subsections.title: Chapter 5: The Climax
subsections.summary: The final confrontation between protagonist and antagonist.
subsections.content:
## Chapter 5: The Reckoning

All threads lead here. The protagonist must apply everything they have learned in a final, defining confrontation...

name: Mutual Nondisclosure Agreement (NDA)
description: Standard agreement for business partnerships requiring confidentiality and asset protection.

sections.title: 1. Preamble & Purpose
sections.summary: Identifies the parties involved, the date of agreement, and the general business purpose.

subsections.title: 1.1 Agreement Title & Date
subsections.summary: Sets official title and date of execution.
subsections.content:
# MUTUAL NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Nondisclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into on this ____ day of ____________, 20__ ("Effective Date"), by and between the parties listed below.

subsections.title: 1.2 Definition of Parties
subsections.summary: Defines who is the Discloser and who is the Recipient.
subsections.content:
### Parties

* **[Disclosing Party Name]**, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of [State/Country] ("Party A").
* **[Receiving Party Name]**, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of [State/Country] ("Party B").

sections.title: 2. Confidential Information
sections.summary: Defines what constitutes confidential information and sets the boundaries of protection.

subsections.title: 2.1 Definition of Protected Information
subsections.summary: Detailed explanation of assets, technical parameters, and code protected.
subsections.content:
### Clause 2.1: Protected Scope

"Confidential Information" refers to any proprietary details, including but not limited to source code, algorithms, business plans, financial projections, customer datasets, and architectural models, marked as confidential or reasonably understood to be proprietary.

subsections.title: 2.2 Permitted Exclusions
subsections.summary: Outlines standard legal exclusions (e.g., publicly available data).
subsections.content:
### Clause 2.2: Standard Exclusions

Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach by Recipient; (b) was already known; or (c) is independently developed.

sections.title: 3. Terms & Jurisdictions
sections.summary: Defines duration of confidentiality obligation, governing law, and dispute resolutions.

subsections.title: 3.1 Term of Obligation
subsections.summary: Sets the number of years the protection lasts.
subsections.content: The obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall survive for a period of **[e.g., 3 years / 5 years]** from the Effective Date of this Agreement.

subsections.title: 3.2 Governing Law
subsections.summary: Specifies the state/country jurisdiction.
subsections.content:
### Clause 3.2: Jurisdiction and Dispute Resolution

This Agreement shall be governed by, construed, and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of **[State Name]**, without regard to conflict of law principles.

name: Standard Software Design Specification
description: A robust template for detailing system architecture, API schemas, and deployment plans.

sections.title: 1. Executive Summary & Goals
sections.summary: Introduction, scope, technical problem description, and defined constraints.

subsections.title: 1.1 System Objectives
subsections.summary: Why are we building this system? What core problems does it solve?
subsections.content:
# Software Design Specification: [System Name]

## 1.1 Objectives & Problem Statement

Describe the business context and technical challenges that require this implementation. What are the key success metrics?

subsections.title: 1.2 System Scope & Out of Scope
subsections.summary: Boundaries of the project implementation.
subsections.content:
## 1.2 System Boundaries

* **In Scope**: List the primary components, features, or APIs being developed.
* **Out of Scope**: Explicitly document what is deliberately excluded from this development phase.

sections.title: 2. Architecture & Data Structures
sections.summary: Detailed design of database tables, network protocols, component diagrams.

subsections.title: 2.1 High-Level Component Architecture
subsections.summary: Overview diagram representation description.
subsections.content:
## 2.1 Component Diagram & Flow

```
[Client Layer] --> (API Gateway) --> [Microservice A] --> (Database)
                                --> [Microservice B] --> (Cache)
```

Explain the interaction between the frontend, backend gateways, worker nodes, and databases.

subsections.title: 2.2 API Endpoints and Schema
subsections.summary: JSON request/response models and interface routing details.

sections.title: 3. Reliability, Security & Scalability
sections.summary: Details on authentication, failure recovery, caching, rate limiting, and compliance.

subsections.title: 3.1 Security & Compliance
subsections.summary: Details on JWT encryption, role-based access control, GDPR requirements.
subsections.content:
## 3.1 Security Specifications

* **Encryption**: TLS 1.3 in-transit, AES-256 at-rest.
* **Authentication**: OAuth 2.0 / JWT token authentication.
* **Access Control**: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) mapping policies.

name: Business Case & Market Analysis Report
description: Comprehensive structure to model business strategies, market opportunities, and financial roadmaps.

sections.title: 1. Executive Summary
sections.summary: High-level synthesis of findings, business model, and strategic proposal.

subsections.title: 1.1 Project Objectives & Goals
subsections.summary: Defines why this report exists and the desired strategic actions.
subsections.content:
# Business Strategy Report: [Topic Name]

## 1.1 Executive Overview

Synthesize the key insights, objectives, and primary recommendations presented in this documentation...

sections.title: 2. Market Analysis & Position
sections.summary: Details on target audiences, competitive landscapes, and SWOT assessments.

subsections.title: 2.1 Competitor Assessment
subsections.summary: Evaluates top industry leaders and market differentiation points.
subsections.content:
## 2.1 Competitor Landscape

| Competitor | Market Share | Strengths | Weaknesses | Our Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Competitor A | 35% | Brand equity | High cost | Cost-leadership |
| Competitor B | 20% | Niche feature | Limited scale | Feature expansion |

subsections.title: 2.2 SWOT Analysis
subsections.summary: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats.
subsections.content:
## 2.2 SWOT Analysis Matrix

* **Strengths**: What distinct assets or core competencies do we possess?
* **Weaknesses**: What parameters limit our execution or scalability?
* **Opportunities**: Which market trends, demographic changes, or technology shifts can we capitalize on?
* **Threats**: What external regulatory, economic, or competitive risks do we face?

sections.title: 3. Financial Strategy & Action Plan
sections.summary: Required funding, ROI calculations, and milestone projection.

subsections.title: 3.1 Action Roadmap & KPIs
subsections.summary: Deliverables checklist, timelines, and measurable goals.
subsections.content:
## 3.1 Strategic Action Plan

1. **Phase 1: Research & Discovery** (Months 1-2): Validate audience assumptions.
2. **Phase 2: MVP Development** (Months 3-5): Launch target operations.
3. **Phase 3: Scale & Optimize** (Months 6-12): Customer acquisition.

name: Scientific / Academic Research Structure
description: Standard architecture following the scientific IMRaD method (Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion).

sections.title: 1. Abstract & Introduction
sections.summary: The scientific objective, paper abstract, governing theories, and literature background.

subsections.title: 1.1 Abstract
subsections.summary: High-level synthesis of findings, methodology, and scientific importance.
subsections.content:
# Research Paper: [Title of the Research]

## Abstract

Provide a concise summary of the research questions, methodologies applied, primary findings, and broader scientific significance (approx. 150-250 words)...

subsections.title: 1.2 Context & Literature Review
subsections.summary: Governing context, hypotheses, and citing relevant scientific research backgrounds.
subsections.content:
## 1.2 Introduction & Background

State the research problem clearly, formulate the primary hypotheses, and discuss relevant historical literature or theories that justify this study...

sections.title: 2. Materials & Methodology
sections.summary: Scientific datasets, experimental cohorts, physical equipment, and formulas applied.

subsections.title: 2.1 Experimental Procedures
subsections.summary: Chronological timeline of tests, cohort sizes, control factors.
subsections.content:
## 2.1 Experimental Setup & cohorts

Detail the material specifications, selection criteria for study cohorts, software environments, and specific algorithms utilized so that the experiment is fully reproducible.

sections.title: 3. Results & Academic Discussion
sections.summary: Data models, numerical outcomes, graphs, discussions of error margins, and summaries.

subsections.title: 3.1 Empirical Findings
subsections.summary: Tabulated outputs, margin of errors, statistical tests.
subsections.content:
## 3.1 Data Analysis & Findings

Present your collected scientific data using tables, equations, and concise descriptive summaries...

subsections.title: 3.2 Scientific Discussion & Limitations
subsections.summary: Interpreting outcomes, limitations of cohorts, future works.
subsections.content:
## 3.2 Discussion

Analyze whether the empirical outcomes validate or reject the initial hypotheses. Discuss any sources of error, limitations in cohort selection, and fields for future research.
